text: The SI unit of electric current is:
explanation: The SI base unit of electric current is the Ampere (A). It is one of the 7 fundamental SI units.
name: SI Unit of Current

text: The dimensional formula of force is:
name: Dimensional Formula of Force

text: The number of significant figures in 0.00450 is:
explanation: Leading zeros are not significant. In 0.00450: 4, 5, 0 (trailing zero after decimal) = 3 significant figures.
name: Significant Figures

text: Which of the following pairs has the same dimensional formula?
explanation: Impulse = \( F \cdot t = [MLT^{-1}] \). Momentum = \( mv = [MLT^{-1}] \). Both have the same dimensions.
name: Same Dimensions

text: The dimensional formula of Planck's constant \(h\) (where \(E = h\nu\)) is:
name: Dimensions of Planck's Constant

text: In the measurement \( 2.35 \pm 0.01 \) cm, the relative error is:
name: Relative Error

text: The method of dimensions cannot be used to:
explanation: Dimensional analysis cannot determine dimensionless constants (like \(\frac{1}{2}\) in \(KE = \frac{1}{2}mv^2\) or \(2\pi\) in \(T = 2\pi\sqrt{l/g}\)).
name: Limitations of Dimensional Analysis

text: Light year is a unit of:
explanation: Light year = distance travelled by light in 1 year \( \approx 9.46 \times 10^{15} \) m. It is a unit of distance (length).
name: Light Year

text: The dimensional formula of pressure is the same as that of:
explanation: Pressure = Force/Area = \( [MLT^{-2}]/[L^2] = [ML^{-1}T^{-2}] \). Stress = Force/Area → same dimensions.
name: Pressure and Stress Dimensions

text: If \( Z = \frac{A^2 B^{1/3}}{C} \) and percentage errors in \(A\), \(B\), \(C\) are 2%, 3%, 1% respectively, the percentage error in \(Z\) is:
name: Error Propagation

text: The accuracy of a Vernier calliper with least count 0.1 mm is:
explanation: Least count of Vernier calliper = 1 MSD − 1 VSD. Typical value: 0.1 mm = 0.01 cm. This is the smallest measurement it can make.
name: Vernier Calliper Least Count

text: The SI unit of luminous intensity is:
explanation: Candela (cd) is the SI base unit of luminous intensity. Lumen is luminous flux, Lux is illuminance.
name: SI Unit of Luminous Intensity

text: Which physical quantity has the unit \( \text{kg m}^2\text{s}^{-2} \)?
explanation: Joule (J) = kg m² s⁻². This is the SI unit of energy (and work and torque).
name: Unit of Energy

text: The parallax method is used to measure:
explanation: Parallax method: used for measuring very large distances like distances of stars (parsec method). Parallax angle \( \theta \), distance \( D = b/\theta \).
name: Parallax Method
